channel.title: Code Defence Intelligence Brief — Wed, 1 Apr 2026
channel.description: Daily cybersecurity intelligence for Wed, 1 Apr 2026.

item.title: North Korean hackers hijack Axios npm package in massive supply chain hit. 🛡️
item.description:
One of the world most trusted HTTP client libraries has been turned into a delivery mechanism for North Korean malware. 🛡️
Supply Chain Alert · Axios npm package poisoned by UNC1069 ❨North Korean APT❩.
In the last 24 hours‚ @[Google](urn:li:organization:1441) and independent researchers confirmed that the Axios npm package was compromised. Attackers hijacked the maintainer account and released two poisoned versions ❨1.14.1 and 0.30.4❩. These versions included a hidden dependency called plain-crypto-js which immediately executes a Remote Access Trojan ❨RAT❩ upon installation.
The malware targets Windows‚ Linux‚ and macOS to exfiltrate cloud access keys‚ SSH tokens‚ and database credentials. Because Axios is used in millions of automated build pipelines‚ this attack provides the adversary with potential reach into the heart of global production environments. UNC1069 is known for targeting the financial and cryptocurrency sectors to fund state programs.
The uncomfortable truth: Your automated build systems trust your dependencies implicitly; a single compromised account on @[GitHub](urn:li:organization:19041) or npm can weaponize your entire infrastructure in seconds.
→ Check project lockfiles for Axios 1.14.1 or 0.30.4 and downgrade immediately. → Rotate all cloud access keys ❨AWS‚ Azure‚ GCP❩‚ NPM tokens‚ and database credentials present on affected systems. → Scope incident response to include CI/CD runners and build infrastructure‚ not just developer workstations.
#Cybersecurity #SupplyChain #Axios #DevSecOps #NorthKorea #CodeDefence

item.title: Google fixes fourth Chrome zero-day exploited in active attacks this year. 🛑
item.description:
Attackers are leveraging a high-severity flaw in the browser CSS component to achieve remote code execution. 🛑
CVE-2026-2441 · Severity High · Active Exploitation in Google Chrome.
@[Google](urn:li:organization:1441) has released an emergency security update to patch a high-severity use-after-free vulnerability in the Chrome CSS component. The company confirmed that an exploit for this flaw exists in the wild. Attackers can trigger the vulnerability by enticing a user to visit a maliciously crafted website.
If successfully exploited‚ the flaw allows for arbitrary code execution within the browser sandbox. While a secondary sandbox escape is typically required for full system takeover‚ this vulnerability is highly effective for session hijacking and stealing data directly from the browser memory. This marks the fourth zero-day patched in Chrome since January 2026.
The uncomfortable truth: Your browser is the most frequently targeted and volatile entry point in your enterprise fleet.
→ Update @[Google](urn:li:organization:1441) Chrome to version 145.0.7632.75/76 for Windows/Mac immediately. → Enforce browser isolation or Zero Trust gateways for high-risk users to neutralize web-based exploits. → Audit your network for anomalous outbound traffic originating from browser processes.
#Cybersecurity #Chrome #ZeroDay #PatchManagement #SOC #CodeDefence

item.title: CISA adds Aqua Security Trivy supply chain breach to KEV catalog. 🐛
item.description:
The scanner you trust to find vulnerabilities has been added to the government list of known security failures. 🐛
CVE-2026-33634 · Severity Critical · CISA KEV Remediation Deadline: April 09‚ 2026.
The @[CISA](urn:li:organization:13010360) has added the embedded malicious code vulnerability in Aqua Security Trivy to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. This follows the industrial-scale supply chain attack by TeamPCP where malicious releases were used to siphon CI/CD tokens and cloud credentials.
Researchers at @[Wiz](urn:li:organization:27083049) have reported that TeamPCP is now explicitly collaborating with the Lapsus$ extortion group‚ acting as an Initial Access Broker ❨IAB❩. They are rapidly validating stolen cloud secrets and selling access to the highest bidder within hours of exfiltration.
The uncomfortable truth: When your security tools are compromised‚ they become the primary vehicle for high-velocity exfiltration and immediate extortion.
→ Apply all vendor-provided mitigations and ensure you are running verified releases of Trivy. → Conduct a mandatory rotation of all CI/CD secrets‚ AWS keys‚ and SSH tokens used in environments where Trivy was executed in the last 14 days. → Transition to pinning all third-party GitHub Actions to full SHA hashes to prevent tag-based hijacking.
#Cybersecurity #SupplyChain #Trivy #TeamPCP #CISA #CodeDefence

item.title: Apple patches multiple zero-days in iOS and macOS tied to spyware kits. 🐚
item.description:
Your mobile fleet is currently being targeted by web-based exploit kits capable of memory corruption. 🐚
Vulnerability Alert · Impacting Apple Safari‚ iOS‚ iPadOS‚ and macOS.
@[Apple](urn:li:organization:162479) has released emergency updates to address multiple vulnerabilities being exploited by mobile spyware kits like DarkSword. CVE-2025-31277 is a critical buffer overflow in WebKit that allows maliciously crafted web content to execute arbitrary code. CVE-2025-43510 is an improper locking flaw that allows apps to cause unexpected changes in shared memory.
These flaws are core components of automated cyberespionage frameworks designed to exfiltrate passwords‚ health data‚ and crypto wallet credentials from high-value targets. The remediation deadline for federal agencies is fast approaching on April 3‚ 2026.
The uncomfortable truth: Your mobile device is a high-value surveillance target that can be compromised simply by loading a website in a vulnerable browser.
→ Force a security update for all managed @[Apple](urn:li:organization:162479) devices to the latest iOS 26.x and macOS 16.x releases today. → Enable Lockdown Mode for high-risk executives to specifically neutralize WebKit-based exploitation paths. → Utilize MDM to block unpatched devices from accessing sensitive corporate applications.
#Cybersecurity #MobileSecurity #Apple #Spyware #ThreatIntelligence #CodeDefence
